creator: joel@tahoestores.com
text:
Yes!

I've been unable to build the server port since the changes to protobuf. I submitted a bug report at the time and was told CND. I was forced to use the pkg. 

After the openssl-3 updates I was once again unable to update the port. In addition I was also unable to update the pgk due to openssl dependencies.

So now I have 

mysql80-server-8.0.32_3 is vulnerable:
  MySQL -- Multiple vulnerabilities
  CVE: CVE-2023-38545
  CVE: CVE-2023-22115
  CVE: CVE-2023-22114
  CVE: CVE-2023-22113
  CVE: CVE-2023-22112
  CVE: CVE-2023-22111
  CVE: CVE-2023-22110
  CVE: CVE-2023-22104
  CVE: CVE-2023-22103
  CVE: CVE-2023-22102
  CVE: CVE-2023-22097
  CVE: CVE-2023-22095
  CVE: CVE-2023-22094
  CVE: CVE-2023-22092
  CVE: CVE-2023-22084
  CVE: CVE-2023-22079
  CVE: CVE-2023-22078
  CVE: CVE-2023-22070
  CVE: CVE-2023-22068
  CVE: CVE-2023-22066
  CVE: CVE-2023-22065
  CVE: CVE-2023-22064
  CVE: CVE-2023-22059
  CVE: CVE-2023-22032
  CVE: CVE-2023-22028
  CVE: CVE-2023-22026
  CVE: CVE-2023-22015
  CVE: CVE-2023-3817
  CVE: CVE-2023-2650
  CVE: CVE-2022-42898
  WWW: https://vuxml.FreeBSD.org/freebsd/22df5074-71cd-11ee-85eb-84a93843eb75.html

All because of

ld-elf.so.1: /usr/ports/databases/mysql80-server/work/.build/library_output_directory/libprotoc.so.3.19.4: Undefined symbol "_ZN6google8protobuf7Message17CopyWithSizeCheckEPS1_RKS1_"

I have a FreeBsd upgrade pending and it would be super nice if this issue was resolved per FreeBsd guidelines which guarantee all ports build.

Is there a new bug report? I'm headed there next.
